title: In My Solitary Hours in My Dear Husband his Absence by Anne Bradstreet — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
O Lord, Thou hear'st my daily moan
And see'st my dropping tears.
My troubles all are Thee before,
My longings and my fears.

Thou hitherto hast been my God;
Thy help my soul hath found.
Though loss and sickness me assailed,
meaning: "In My Solitary Hours in My Dear Husband his Absence" by Anne Bradstreet is a sad and love and deep poem consisting of 64 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love and deep through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "O Lord, Thou hear'st my daily moan And see'st my dropping tears....", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Anne Bradstreet's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
